title: Yield-bearing stablecoin slowdown ends three-year run for crypto-native products
summary: Yield-bearing stablecoin slowdown ends three-year run for crypto-native products
briefing: Yield-bearing stablecoin supply fell 15% in Q2 as sUSDe and sUSDS contracted, while Treasury-backed products including BUIDL, USYC and USDY continued to grow.

title: OFAC sanctions 134 ISIS-K crypto wallet addresses as Tether freezes funds
summary: OFAC sanctions 134 ISIS-K crypto wallet addresses as Tether freezes funds
briefing: OFAC added 134 crypto wallet addresses linked to ISIS-K to its sanctions list, including 131 Tron addresses that received more than $1.4 million in crypto donations, according to Chainalysis.

title: Taiko reopens bridge after $1.7M exploit, says users made whole
summary: Taiko reopens bridge after $1.7M exploit, says users made whole
briefing: Taiko restored bridge transfers after replenishing asset backing and completing security fixes following an 11-day network disruption.

title: Metaplanet buys 2,823 BTC, surpasses 43,000 in Bitcoin holdings
summary: Metaplanet buys 2,823 BTC, surpasses 43,000 in Bitcoin holdings
briefing: Metaplanet bought 2,823 Bitcoin during the second quarter, reducing its average acquisition cost to $106,500 per BTC, and reported $10.9 million in revenue from its income generation strategy.

title: OpenAI weighs 5% stake for US government amid Trump talks: FT
summary: OpenAI weighs 5% stake for US government amid Trump talks: FT
briefing: OpenAI has reportedly discussed giving the US government a 5% equity stake in early Trump administration talks as Washington tightens AI model oversight.

title: Robinhood links with dYdX Labs to launch new DEX Arcus
summary: Robinhood links with dYdX Labs to launch new DEX Arcus
briefing: The crypto protocol dYdX has rebranded as Arcus and is joining Robinhood’s new blockchain to bring perpetual and tokenized stock trading.

title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Here’s what happened in crypto today
briefing: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: France to strengthen response as crypto wrench attacks hit 77
summary: France to strengthen response as crypto wrench attacks hit 77
briefing: French Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez promised a “more ambitious” three-part plan to reinforce security measures for the crypto sector.

title: Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to US Over $8M Crypto Ransom Demand
summary: Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to US Over $8M Crypto Ransom Demand
briefing: The teenager allegedly helped to breach a luxury jeweler and demand $8 million in crypto, as part of a crew tied to $100 million in ransoms.

title: Robinhood Launches 'AI-Native' Ethereum Layer-2 Network, Tokenized Stock Trading
summary: Robinhood Launches 'AI-Native' Ethereum Layer-2 Network, Tokenized Stock Trading
briefing: Brokerage and crypto exchange Robinhood opened the public mainnet for its Arbitrum-powered Ethereum layer-2 network on Wednesday.

title: Venice AI Valued at $1 Billion as Erik Voorhees Makes the Case for Private ChatGPT Rivals
summary: Venice AI Valued at $1 Billion as Erik Voorhees Makes the Case for Private ChatGPT Rivals
briefing: Venice reached a $1 billion valuation as founder Erik Voorhees argued AI companies should protect users' conversations.

title: LongCat-2.0: The Stealth AI Model That Was Quietly Topping OpenRouter All Along
summary: LongCat-2.0: The Stealth AI Model That Was Quietly Topping OpenRouter All Along
briefing: The 1.6 trillion-parameter mixture-of-experts model spent two months disguised as "Owl Alpha" before Meituan claimed it—and it undercuts GPT-5.5 and Claude Sonnet 5 on price by a wide margin.

title: UN's First AI Safety Panel Says Scientists Can't Rule Out 'Catastrophic Harm'
summary: UN's First AI Safety Panel Says Scientists Can't Rule Out 'Catastrophic Harm'
briefing: A 40-scientist panel found that AI capabilities are outrunning both scientific understanding and government oversight.

title: Trump-Backed American Bitcoin Hits New Low Price Ahead of Reverse Stock Split
summary: Trump-Backed American Bitcoin Hits New Low Price Ahead of Reverse Stock Split
briefing: Bitcoin mining firm American Bitcoin fell to a new low price Wednesday, one day before executing a 1-for-15 reverse stock split.

title: BitMine, Sharplink and Joe Lubin Accelerate Wall Street Ethereum Push With Nonprofit Launch
summary: BitMine, Sharplink and Joe Lubin Accelerate Wall Street Ethereum Push With Nonprofit Launch
briefing: Ethereum Institutional will serve as a point of contact for financial institutions seeking to get more involved with on-chain infrastructure.

title: CRCL Sell-Off 'Looks Overdone' Say Analysts as Circle CEO Addresses Open USD Threat
summary: CRCL Sell-Off 'Looks Overdone' Say Analysts as Circle CEO Addresses Open USD Threat
briefing: Analysts don't think the threat of a new stablecoin launch was enough to warrant a major Circle (CRCL) sell-off on Tuesday.
